lesson.heading: Short Vowel Sounds
lesson.body: Short vowel sounds are quick sounds: a like in cat, e like in bed, i like in pig, o like in hop, u like in sun. Practice saying these words out loud: cat, bed, pig, hop, sun.

lesson.heading: Mini Reading Passage: The Lost Cap
lesson.body: Sam had a red cap. The wind took his cap! Sam ran to get it. The cap was on a big rock. Sam was glad to get his cap back.

lesson.heading: Reading Check
lesson.body: Talk about the story together: What color was Sam's cap? What happened to the cap? Where did Sam find it?

exercises.prompt: There are 6 red apples and 7 green apples in a basket. How many apples in all?

writingPrompt.prompt: Write 1 to 2 sentences about your favorite hat, toy, or object. Remember to start with a capital letter and end with a period.

vocabulary.definition: A small furry pet that says meow.
A piece of furniture you sleep on.
To jump on one or both feet.
The bright star that lights up our sky in the day.
A farm animal that says oink.

quizQuestions.question: What color was Sam's cap?
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says Sam had a red cap.

quizQuestions.question: What is 7 + 5?
The word 'sun' has a short u sound.
quizQuestions.explanation: Sun has the short u sound, like in 'cup'.

quizQuestions.question: The wind took Sam's cap away in the story.
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says the wind took his cap.

quizQuestions.question: What is 9 + 6?

parentChecklist.label: Read 'The Lost Cap' together out loud
Checked the addition problems for accuracy
Listened to the sentence about a favorite object
Practiced spelling the 5 words together

lesson.heading: Long Vowel Sounds
lesson.body: Long vowel sounds say their own name: a like in cake, e like in bee, i like in bike, o like in boat, u like in cube. Practice saying these words out loud: cake, bee, bike, boat, cube.

lesson.heading: Mini Reading Passage: The Bike Ride
lesson.body: Mia rode her bike down the lane. She saw a bee near a flower. Mia did not stop. She rode all the way to the lake and back home.

lesson.heading: Reading Check
lesson.body: Talk about the story together: What did Mia ride? What did she see near a flower? Where did she ride to?

exercises.prompt: Mia had 15 stickers. She gave 6 to her friend. How many does she have left?

writingPrompt.prompt: Fix these sentences so they start with a capital letter and end with the right punctuation: (1) i like to ride my bike (2) did you see the bee (3) the lake was so much fun

vocabulary.definition: A sweet baked dessert, often eaten on birthdays.
A flying insect that makes honey.
A two-wheeled vehicle you pedal to move.
A vehicle that travels on water.
A solid shape with six equal square sides.

quizQuestions.question: What did Mia ride in the story?
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says Mia rode her bike down the lane.

quizQuestions.question: What is 14 - 6?
The word 'boat' has a long o sound.
quizQuestions.explanation: Boat has the long o sound, like the letter name 'o'.

quizQuestions.question: Mia stopped riding when she saw the bee.
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says Mia did not stop.

quizQuestions.question: What is 18 - 9?

parentChecklist.label: Read 'The Bike Ride' together out loud
Checked the subtraction problems for accuracy
Reviewed corrected sentences for capitals and punctuation
Practiced spelling the 5 words together

title: Adventure Continues: Tens and Ones Outpost

lesson.heading: Place Value: Tens and Ones
lesson.body: Every two-digit number has tens and ones. In the number 34, the 3 means 3 tens (30) and the 4 means 4 ones. 30 + 4 = 34. Practice breaking apart numbers into tens and ones.

lesson.heading: Mini Reading Passage: The Garden Helper
lesson.body: Dev helped his grandma in the garden. They planted 2 rows of carrots. Each row had 10 carrot seeds. Dev counted all the seeds carefully.

lesson.heading: Reading Check
lesson.body: Talk about the story together: Who did Dev help? What did they plant? How many rows of carrots were there?

exercises.prompt: How many tens and ones are in 47?
How many tens and ones are in 62?
Write the number that has 5 tens and 3 ones.
Write the number that has 8 tens and 0 ones.
Dev planted 2 rows of 10 carrot seeds. How many seeds in all?

writingPrompt.prompt: Write 2 to 3 sentences about a time you helped someone, like Dev helped his grandma.

vocabulary.definition: A place where plants, flowers, or vegetables are grown.
A small part of a plant that can grow into a new plant.
A straight line of things, like plants or seats.
To find the total number of something.
An orange vegetable that grows underground.

quizQuestions.question: How many tens are in the number 47?
Who did Dev help in the garden?
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says Dev helped his grandma in the garden.

quizQuestions.question: The number 62 has 6 tens and 2 ones.
Dev and his grandma planted only 1 row of carrots.
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says they planted 2 rows.

quizQuestions.question: How many seeds were planted in all (2 rows of 10)?

parentChecklist.label: Read 'The Garden Helper' together out loud
Practiced breaking numbers into tens and ones
Listened to the 2-3 sentence writing about helping someone
Practiced spelling the 5 words together

title: Adventure Continues: Clock and Coin Camp

lesson.heading: Time and Money Basics
lesson.body: A clock has an hour hand (short) and a minute hand (long). When the minute hand points to 12, it is exactly on the hour, like 3:00. A penny is worth 1 cent, a nickel is worth 5 cents, a dime is worth 10 cents, and a quarter is worth 25 cents.

lesson.heading: Mini Reading Passage: Saving Up
lesson.body: Lily wanted a new book. The book cost 50 cents. She saved 2 quarters in her piggy bank. Now Lily has enough money to buy the book!

lesson.heading: Reading Check
lesson.body: Talk about the story together: What did Lily want to buy? How much did it cost? How did she save enough money?

exercises.prompt: If the short hand points to 4 and the long hand points to 12, what time is it?
How many cents is 1 quarter worth?
How many cents are 2 dimes and 1 nickel worth together?
Draw or describe a clock showing 7:00.
Lily saved 2 quarters. How many cents is that in all?

writingPrompt.prompt: Fix these sentences: (1) lily saved her money (2) how much does it cost (3) i want to save money too

vocabulary.definition: A tool that tells us what time it is.
A coin worth 1 cent.
A coin worth 10 cents.
A coin worth 25 cents.
To keep something, like money, for later.

quizQuestions.question: How much is a quarter worth?
What did Lily want to buy?
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says Lily wanted a new book.

quizQuestions.question: A dime is worth 10 cents.
Lily did not have enough money to buy the book.
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says she saved enough to buy the book.

quizQuestions.question: How many cents are 2 quarters worth together?

parentChecklist.label: Read 'Saving Up' together out loud
Practiced telling time and counting coins
Reviewed corrected sentences for capitals and punctuation
Practiced spelling the 5 words together

lesson.heading: Review Briefing
lesson.body: This week you practiced short and long vowels, addition and subtraction within 20, tens and ones, and time and money. Today's mission mixes a little bit of everything!

lesson.heading: Mini Reading Passage: The Big Picnic
lesson.body: Our class had a picnic. We sat in 2 rows. Each row had 10 kids. We ate at noon, when the clock showed 12:00. Everyone had a fun, sunny day.

lesson.heading: Reading Check
lesson.body: Talk about the story together: How many rows were there? How many kids in each row? What time did they eat?

exercises.prompt: How many tens and ones are in 53?
How many cents are 3 dimes worth?
If 2 rows have 10 kids each, how many kids in all?

writingPrompt.prompt: Write 2 to 3 sentences about your favorite part of this week's learning.

vocabulary.definition: A meal eaten outdoors, often on a blanket.
Twelve o'clock in the middle of the day.
A straight line of things, like seats or plants (review).
To keep something for later (review).

quizQuestions.question: What is 9 + 8?
What time did the class eat at the picnic?
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says they ate at noon, when the clock showed 12:00.

quizQuestions.question: There were 3 rows of kids at the picnic.
quizQuestions.explanation: The story says there were 2 rows.

quizQuestions.question: How many tens are in the number 53?

parentChecklist.label: Read 'The Big Picnic' together out loud
Checked the mixed review exercises for accuracy
Listened to the reflection writing
Celebrated finishing Week 1!
